sections.title: Market Pulse: A Look Back at Friday's Session

items.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Close at Record Highs; Dow Slips
items.summary: U.S. equity markets concluded the week with mixed performance on Friday, April 25, 2026, as the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite surged to new all-time highs. Investor sentiment was largely buoyed by strong performances in the technology and semiconductor sectors, fueled by ongoing enthusiasm for artificial intelligence and positive corporate earnings. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, however, experienced a slight decline. Geopolitical tensions, including a security scare involving Donald Trump and continued concerns in the Middle East, introduced some volatility throughout the trading week, though bullish momentum in growth sectors largely prevailed.

items.headline: Intel Surges on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Intel Corporation ($INTC) saw a massive surge of 23.6% to $82.55, marking its best single day since 1987. This impressive gain was driven by the company's strong first-quarter revenue and earnings per share, which significantly beat consensus expectations.

items.headline: Nvidia Extends AI-Driven Rally
items.summary: Nvidia Corporation ($NVDA) continued its upward trajectory, closing up 4.32% and retaking a $5 trillion market capitalization. The ongoing demand for artificial intelligence and semiconductors remains a primary catalyst for the stock's performance.

items.headline: ServiceNow Experiences Significant Drop
items.summary: ServiceNow ($NOW) faced an 18% earnings collapse on Thursday, contributing to its decline. Concerns about AI/SaaS cannibalization fears in the software sector have impacted investor confidence.

items.headline: Tesla Shares Decline Despite Earnings Beat
items.summary: Tesla ($TSLA) shares experienced a sell-off despite beating first-quarter revenue and earnings expectations. Analysis revealed that a significant portion of the boosted profits came from one-time reliefs related to warranty and tariffs, raising questions about underlying performance.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Poised for Fed Chair Amid Independence Pledge
items.summary: Kevin Warsh's path to becoming the next Fed Chair appears clearer after the Department of Justice dropped its probe into Jerome Powell, removing a major hurdle. During his confirmation hearing, Warsh emphasized his commitment to independence and hinted at a 'different, new inflation framework,' suggesting a potential shift in monetary policy approach. The next FOMC decision, expected April 28-29, is widely anticipated to result in a hold on interest rates, with a 99.5% probability priced in by futures.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Reveals Mixed Signals
items.summary: Recent economic data presented a mixed picture. The University of Michigan's final April sentiment index recorded its lowest reading since 1952 at 49.8, with year-ahead inflation expectations at 4.7%. Conversely, pending home sales rose by 1.5% month-over-month. The April flash composite PMI hit 52.0, with manufacturing reaching its highest level since May 2022 at 54.0, but deteriorating supplier delivery times indicated potential 'stagflation' rather than reacceleration. Initial jobless claims increased to 214,000.

items.headline: Technology and AI Continue to Drive Market Leadership
items.summary: The technology and semiconductor sectors continued to demonstrate robust leadership, particularly in the month of April. From April 1 to April 21, the tech sector was the best-performing S&P 500 sector, up over 15%, reversing a challenging first quarter. This rebound is largely attributed to strong demand for artificial intelligence and related computing infrastructure.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Add Layer of Uncertainty
items.summary: Geopolitical events continued to be a source of market volatility. A security scare involving Donald Trump and ongoing tensions in the Middle East, including the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, are contributing to investor anxiety. Oil prices continue to move higher due to the Strait of Hormuz disruption, impacting inflation expectations.
